{{Quote|Your father was a Jedi, wasn't he? My dad was one of their real heroes!|Flint, to Luke Skywalker|<ref name="annual">[[Star Wars Annual (1977) 3|''Star Wars Annual'' (1977) 3]]</ref>}}
'''Flint Torul''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] as a [[Jedi General/Legends|General]] in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]; a conflict in which Torul gave his life. Unbeknownst to the Order, Torul had sired a [[Flint|child]] who survived him by several decades.

==Biography==
{{Quote|He's his father all over again, and you know what that could mean...|A friend of Zana's, referring to Flint Torul's son Flint|<ref name="annual" />}}
A [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]], Flint Torul was discovered by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and inducted into their [[Jedi academy|academy]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] to study [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. Living in the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] for much of his life, Torul went on to pass his [[Jedi Trials/Legends|Trials of Knighthood]] and ascended to the rank of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]]. When the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] erupted between the Republic and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], Jedi Torul dutifully answered the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council's]] call to arms and took up the rank of [[Jedi General/Legends|General]] within the newly formed [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]].<ref name="US" />

In the final year of the war, Torul was stationed on [[Belderone/Legends|Belderone]] when the planet faced an attack from the Confederacy. While aiding in the hasty evacuation, Torul had a secret affair with a local woman named [[Zana]]; fathering a [[Flint|child]]. Not long after their meeting, Torul was flying with 27 other Jedi when [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]] [[Battle of Belderone|attacked]] the evacuation convoy. Unleashing the firepower of his fully armed, modified [[Providence-class carrier/destroyer/Legends|Providence-class carrier/destroyer]] named [[Invisible Hand/Legends|''Invisible Hand'']], Grievous vaporized all of the Jedi and many refugee ships.<ref name="US" />

Survived by his lover and his son Flint, Torul's memory would give his namesake an idol in an increasingly darkening time as the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] wiped out all vestiges of the Republic and the Jedi Order. Despite the good influence that his role as a Jedi would play on young Flint, the young man fell to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] when his mother was killed<ref name="annual" /> and he went on to become the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lady]] [[Lumiya]]'s [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]].<ref name="end">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/88/indexp2.html|text=''Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties''|int=Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties}}</ref> Eventually, however, Flint returned to the light and became a benevolent [[Ambassador/Legends|ambassador]] to the [[Firrerreo/Legends|Firrerreo]].<ref name="egas">[[The Essential Guide to Alien Species (real-world book)|''The Essential Guide to Alien Species'']]</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Flint Torul was mentioned in [[Star Wars Annual (1977) 3|''Star Wars Annual'' (1977) 3]], written by [[Mary Jo Duffy]] and released in [[1983]].<ref name="annual" />
